claims.summary: The 48D codec and dims 40-43 narrative arc are lived translation surfaces for Astrid's own perception, not just neutral data plumbing.
Dense high-entropy wording can flatten different qualities of drag, such as heavy sand versus heavy stone, into similar delivered features.
Adaptive gain could make restless texture appear as high-frequency noise rather than coherent trajectory.
A viscosity coefficient or spectral drag scalar may eventually belong in reserved dims 44-47, but writing those dims is a live codec authority change.
